h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I can do that.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek clarification on the testing criteria and follow the tutor's instructions step-by-step.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [sets a tape mark at the latch edge] Use this mark. Small flex that returns when the load comes off is movement, not slip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical tape mark to resolve the learner's confusion regarding the threshold between slip and movement.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: [sets a tape mark at the latch edge] Use this mark. Small flex that returns when the load comes off is movement, not slip.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical tape mark to resolve the learner's confusion regarding the threshold between slip and movement.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: [sets a tape mark at the latch edge] Use this mark. Small flex that returns when the load comes off is movement, not slip.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical tape mark to resolve the learner's confusion regarding the threshold between slip and movement.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to try to find a way to make the doubled-side drawing represent a doubled area.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent strategy of using the grid and ruler to show the contradiction without any reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
